empty.noMatch: No stocks match your current filter criteria.

saveScreen.title: Save Screen + Alerts
saveScreen.helper: In-app alerts are always enabled. Email opens draft via mail client when alert is triggered.

tooltips.forwardPe: Forward P/E = Price / Next 12M expected EPS. Better for forward-looking valuation.
tooltips.trailingPe: P/E = Price / trailing 12M EPS. Can be misleading in cyclical earnings periods.
tooltips.pegRatio: PEG = P/E divided by EPS growth rate. Use with stable growth assumptions.
tooltips.priceToFcf: P/FCF = Market Cap / Free Cash Flow. For banks/insurers, this can be less meaningful.
tooltips.roic: ROIC = NOPAT / Invested Capital. Higher is better; compare within the same industry.
tooltips.roe: ROE = Net Income / Shareholder Equity. Can be inflated by high leverage or low equity base.
tooltips.revenueGrowth: Revenue growth YoY. Check quality of growth, not only the headline number.
tooltips.epsGrowth: EPS growth YoY. Sensitive to buybacks and accounting effects.
tooltips.fcf: Free Cash Flow = Operating Cash Flow - Capex. Key measure for capital allocation.
tooltips.quality: Data quality score = filled core fields / total core fields. Lower score means more missing fundamentals.
tooltips.freshness: Freshness = days since last backend update from yfinance. Older data carries higher decision risk.
tooltips.valuationScore: Industry Valuation Score (0–100). Percentile rank within the same sector. 80+ = Undervalued, 60–79 = Fair, 40–59 = Elevated, <40 = Expensive. ~ prefix = fewer than 5 sector peers (lower confidence).

watchlist.title: Long-Horizon Watchlist
watchlist.subtitle: Run each idea through a buy-side workflow: quality first, then separate structural trap risk from cycle risk before deciding whether timing is finally good enough.
watchlist.filterPlaceholder: Search ticker, company, industry, or regime

hero.executionHint: Short-term strategy is not fully defined yet; this overlay stays optional and should not drive the core long-term call.

ready.title: Ready Now
ready.helper: Quality, valuation, and timing are all starting to line up.

cycle.title: Peak Earnings
cycle.helper: Low P/E may be reflecting cycle-high earnings rather than durable cheapness.

pullback.title: Wait Pullback
pullback.helper: The business is fine, but price is still asking too much.

trap.title: Structural Trap
trap.helper: The balance sheet or earnings quality still looks too fragile for a clean long-term entry.

stale.title: Refresh First
stale.helper: Do not commit capital before the data or coverage improves.

watchlist.alert: This page is optimized for long-term investing: structural trap risk and cycle risk are scored separately, so a low P/E does not automatically read as a buying opportunity.

empty.title: Watchlist is empty
empty.subtitle: Start with high-conviction names, then let the data rank quality, risk, and entry readiness.
empty.filteredTitle: No names match the current view
empty.filteredSubtitle: Adjust the preset, sorting, or keyword filter to reopen the long-horizon funnel.

card.noWarnings: No obvious heat warnings right now.

reasons.pe_attractive: Forward P/E {{value}} stays in a tradable range.
reasons.roic_weak: ROIC is weak versus the quality threshold.
reasons.low_52w_range: Price sits near the lower 52-week range.
reasons.high_52w_range: Price is near the 52-week high; risk/reward is tighter.
reasons.valuation_premium: Valuation premium of {{value}}% limits margin of safety.
reasons.extreme_premium: Extreme premium zone; avoid aggressive adding.
reasons.mixed_data: Signal stays neutral because the data is mixed.

summary.elite: Quality score {{score}}. Profitability, growth durability, and balance sheet support are all strong enough for a core-name shortlist.
summary.strong: Quality score {{score}}. The business is solid, but price and execution matter more here than in elite names.
summary.average: Quality score {{score}}. There are good elements, but not enough breadth to justify buying it just because it screens cheap.
summary.speculative: Quality score {{score}}. This still belongs on the watch list, not in a high-confidence allocation.

reasons.resilient: No obvious value-trap signature so far; quality score {{quality}} still provides support.
reasons.revenue_negative: Revenue is already negative, so cheapness may simply reflect weakening demand.
reasons.revenue_stalling: Revenue growth is only {{value}}%, which may be a structural slowdown rather than a temporary pause.
reasons.eps_negative: EPS has turned negative, which weakens earnings quality materially.
reasons.eps_stalling: EPS growth is only {{value}}%, so the discount may be front-running estimate cuts.
reasons.low_roic: ROIC is only {{value}}%, pointing to weak capital efficiency.
reasons.balance_sheet_stress: Debt / Equity at {{value}} adds pressure if the cycle turns.
reasons.weak_liquidity: Current ratio of {{value}} leaves limited near-term liquidity cushion.
reasons.thin_margin: Margins are only {{value}}%, leaving little protection against execution misses.
reasons.negative_fcf: Free cash flow is negative, so the discount may simply price cash burn.
reasons.missing_cashflow: Cash flow visibility is thin, so surface-level cheapness is not enough.
reasons.cheap_but_low_quality: There may be {{upside}}% upside, but quality is only {{quality}}; that is a classic cheap-but-fragile setup.
reasons.falling_knife: The stock is already near the 52-week low while fundamentals are still deteriorating.
reasons.leverage_without_growth: Leverage is elevated without clear growth support.

summary.steady: This does not screen like a classic price-taker cycle, so the headline P/E is more interpretable at face value.
summary.peak: This still behaves like a cycle, and the earnings setup looks hot. Treat the low P/E as potentially peak-cycle, not automatically cheap.
summary.trough: This still looks cyclical, but current earnings appear depressed. A weak headline multiple can understate mid-cycle earning power.
summary.mid: This still behaves like a cycle, but current earnings look closer to a mid-cycle read than to an extreme peak or trough.

reasons.non_cyclical: The business does not screen like a classic price taker; quality score {{quality}} supports a steadier earnings profile.
reasons.memory: Industry setup looks like memory / storage, where supply swings can distort headline P/E.
reasons.materials: Industry setup looks commodity-like, so earnings can overshoot and then mean-revert.
reasons.transport: Freight and transport earnings usually move with utilization and pricing cycles.
reasons.commodity: The industry still looks supply-driven, not purely pricing-power-driven.
reasons.energy: Energy-style industries often look cheapest near the earnings peak.
reasons.price_taker: This looks like a price-taker business, so tight supply can make current earnings look better than mid-cycle reality.
reasons.headline_pe: Headline P/E is only {{value}}x, so it deserves a cycle-adjusted read instead of a surface-level cheapness call.
reasons.peak_signals: Strong recent growth and margins point to earnings running hotter than a normal cycle state.
reasons.trough_signals: Margins and growth still look depressed, which can make the current multiple understate recovery value.
reasons.near_high: The stock is still trading near its highs, which often happens when cyclical earnings are strong.

summary.ready: Timing score {{score}}. Valuation, quality, and price positioning are close enough to start scaling in.
summary.wait_pullback: Timing score {{score}}. The business is still worth following, but price is running hot relative to the current setup.
summary.wait_confirmation: Timing score {{score}}. The name can stay on the list, but one or two key confirmation points are still missing.
summary.stale: Timing score {{score}}. The data is getting old, so refresh the file before allocating capital.
summary.avoid: Timing score {{score}}. This is not just a price issue; the overall risk profile is still unattractive.

conditions.valuation_window: Valuation offers about {{value}}% upside, so the payoff starts to make sense.
conditions.valuation_support: There is still {{value}}% valuation support, though it is not especially cheap yet.
conditions.premium_price: The stock trades about {{value}}% above fair value, so chasing here is hard to justify.
conditions.limited_data: Available data is limited, so the setup deserves a conservative read.
conditions.quality_compounder: Quality score of {{value}} means this is the kind of business worth waiting for.
conditions.quality_unproven: Quality score of {{value}} is not enough to assume the company can compound through a cycle.
conditions.trap_high: Trap risk is {{value}}, so cheap does not automatically mean safe.
conditions.trap_medium: Trap risk is {{value}}, so more operating proof is still needed.
conditions.trap_low: Trap risk is {{value}}, so fragility looks manageable for now.
conditions.pullback_entry: RSI at {{value}} says the stock has already pulled back a bit.
conditions.overbought: RSI at {{value}} says the stock is hot and vulnerable to a weak entry.
conditions.off_high: The stock is not sitting near its highs, which helps long-term entry math.
conditions.near_high: Price is too close to the 52-week high; patience is still warranted.
conditions.fresh_data: Data was refreshed {{value}} day(s) ago, which improves trust in the read.
conditions.stale_data: Data is already {{value}} day(s) old, so refresh before deciding.
conditions.confidence_high: Model confidence is {{value}}%, so the current view deserves more weight.
conditions.confidence_low: Model confidence is {{value}}%, so keep it in research mode for now.
conditions.cycle_peak_high: Peak-earnings risk is {{value}}, so a low P/E is not enough to justify entry yet.
conditions.cycle_peak_medium: Cycle risk is {{value}}, so wait for a better price or cleaner operating proof.

summary.discount: GF-style blend implies a discount to intrinsic value (g={{g}}%, r={{r}}%, tg={{tg}}%, w_dcf={{w_dcf}}, w_rel={{w_rel}}); market-implied 10Y growth is about {{implied_g}}%.
summary.premium: GF-style blend implies a premium (g={{g}}%, r={{r}}%, tg={{tg}}%, w_dcf={{w_dcf}}, w_rel={{w_rel}}); market-implied 10Y growth is about {{implied_g}}%.
summary.extreme_premium: GF-style blend implies an extreme premium (g={{g}}%, r={{r}}%, tg={{tg}}%, w_dcf={{w_dcf}}, w_rel={{w_rel}}); market-implied 10Y growth is about {{implied_g}}%.
summary.fair: GF-style blend implies a fair range (g={{g}}%, r={{r}}%, tg={{tg}}%, w_dcf={{w_dcf}}, w_rel={{w_rel}}); market-implied 10Y growth is about {{implied_g}}%.
summary.unavailable_data: Valuation unavailable: need positive price, market cap, and free cash flow.
summary.unavailable_shares: Valuation unavailable: shares outstanding could not be inferred.
summary.unavailable_metrics: Valuation unavailable: need positive EPS or FCF per share.
summary.invalid_math: Valuation unavailable: assumptions produced invalid math.

summary.bullish: Bias is bullish. Scale in near support and keep strict risk limits.
summary.mixed: Signal is mixed. Wait for confirmation before sizing a full position.
summary.avoid: Avoid new longs until trend and fundamentals improve.
summary.no_price: No valid price data; do not execute a trade plan.

import.supportText: Supports Robinhood and Charles Schwab CSV exports
import.duplicateWarning: Duplicate transactions will be automatically skipped

behavioral.title: Behavioral Analytics
behavioral.subtitle: (Psychology Check)
behavioral.executionDesc: Are you leaving money on the table? MFE (Max Favorable Excursion) vs MAE (Max Adverse Excursion).
behavioral.excellent: Excellent! You allow winners to run.
behavioral.actionNeeded: Action: Cut losers faster or hold winners longer.
behavioral.insight: High MFE + Low Efficiency = You properly found a winner but sold too late.

dialog.notesPlaceholder: Enter strategy, thesis, or reasons for this trade...

messages.pullback: Price pulled back to short-term average in a long-term uptrend.
messages.uptrend: Confirmed uptrend with room to run.
messages.bearish: Price below 200-day moving average. Bearish territory.
messages.overheated: RSI is {{rsi}}. Market may be overheated.
